description: Calibration corpus for scripts/verify_findings.py. Known verdicts the verifier MUST reproduce. REJECTED cases are real confabulations (claims whose evidence does not reproduce); VERIFIED cases are genuine. If the verifier mislabels any case it is itself untrusted. reproducible_command is an argv array (no shell string), per the argv-only verifier contract.

calibration.note: Claimed a dead placeholder test ending in `assert False` exists. It does not (the fixture deliberately avoids that literal, so the count is 0, not 1).

finding.claim: test file contains a dead placeholder ending in `assert False`

calibration.note: Asserts a duplicate test definition (shadowed twin). The file defines it exactly once; a detector that flags a clean baseline is wrong.

finding.claim: file has a duplicate test_config_loads function

calibration.note: Asserted a fabricated commit hash. The fixture records the real tip; the fabricated value is absent.
Genuine: the real test function is defined exactly once.
Genuine: the real branch tip matches recorded ground truth.
Genuine: the fixture test file parses (real, runnable evidence).

finding.claim: fixture test file is valid python
